mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer demand that supports corporate revenue expansion and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive monthly changes in RSAFS are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household income and depress equity valuations.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate combined with a contraction in industrial production indicates restrictive monetary policy is transmitting into the real economy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month and INDPRO decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising FEDFUNDS and falling INDPRO fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or the interaction term has a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, an acceleration in consumer prices forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy faster than markets expect, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following CPIAUCSL acceleration above 0.4% are positive in more than 55% of observations during rising inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects manufacturing sector strength and capacity utilization gains that translate into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly positive momentum in INDPRO is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above its trailing four-quarter average signals aggregate demand outperformance that lifts earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 1-quarter growth exceeds trailing 4-quarter average growth
failure_condition: 20-day S&P 500 forward returns following above-trend GDPC1 growth are negative in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and durable goods demand that precedes broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day S&P 500 forward returns following month-over-month declines in HOUST are positive in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% reflects sufficiently restrictive monetary policy that raises short-term borrowing costs and compresses equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.
